intro: Ubisoft co-founder Claude Guillemot died in a plane crash in France at age 69; he built the company behind major franchises including Assassin's Creed and Far Cry. Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving Google DeepMind to join Anthropic, continuing a broader exodus of senior researchers from the search giant. A new study found that Claude AI outperformed professional fundraisers and elite debaters in persuasion tasks, raising concerns about AI's growing influence over human decision-making.

whitelist.title: Ubisoft co-founder Claude Guillemot dies in plane crash in France
whitelist.desc: Guillemot worked largely behind the scenes at the video game giant that published titles like the Assassin’s Creed franchise.

whitelist.title: This LLM in a box is powered by a hand crank.
